annex_b_fair_play_contest.note: Annexe B governs the separate FIFA Fair Play Contest award. It is NOT the tie-break conduct score; the ranking tie-break uses the team conduct score defined inside Article 13 step 2 criterion f.
annex_b_fair_play_contest.title: Annexe B: Fair Play Contest Regulations

annex_c_third_place_combinations.note: Each option row assigns one third-placed group token (3A-3L) to each of the eight group winners that face a best third-placed team in the round of 32. Printed page numbers end at 84; the table continues to option 495 on unnumbered physical pages 85-98.
annex_c_third_place_combinations.referenced_by: Article 12.6 note, printed page 24: 'Annexe C of these Regulations includes the 495 different possible combinations of the eight best-ranked third-placed teams and their next match-up for the round of 32, at the completion of the group stage.'
annex_c_third_place_combinations.title: Annexe C: Combinations for eight best third-placed teams

best_third_ranking.article: Article 13, section 'The eight best-ranked teams among those finishing third (third-placed teams from 1 to 8)'
criteria_order: a) greatest number of points obtained in all group matches
b) goal difference resulting from all group matches
c) greatest number of goals scored in all group matches
d) highest team conduct score per Article 13 paragraph 1 step 2 criterion f
e) most recent published edition of the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking
f) immediately preceding published editions of the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking continually until a decision can be made

fair_play_conduct_score.note: Only one deduction is applied to a player or team official in a single match. The team with the highest conduct score ranks highest. Annexe B's Fair Play Contest is a separate award and is not part of group ranking.

steps.step_1: Among the teams concerned: a) greatest number of points in group matches between the teams concerned; b) superior goal difference in group matches between the teams concerned; c) greatest number of goals scored in all group matches between the teams concerned.
steps.step_2: Criteria a)-c) reapplied to matches between the remaining tied teams only; if no decision: d) superior goal difference in all group matches; e) greatest number of goals scored in all group matches; f) highest team conduct score (yellow -1, indirect red -3, direct red -4, yellow + direct red -5). The second step does not restart for teams remaining after a criterion is applied.
steps.step_3: g) most recent published edition of the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking; h) immediately preceding published editions of the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking continually until a decision can be made.

group_tie_break.title: Equal points and qualification for knockout stages

knockout_resolution.summary: If level after normal time in knockout stages: two 15-minute extra-time periods; if still level, penalties (penalty shoot-out) per the Laws of the Game.
knockout_resolution.title: Extra time and penalties (penalty shoot-out)

round_of_32_pairings.summary: Top two of each of the 12 groups plus the eight best third-placed teams qualify for the round of 32. Article 12.6 lists matches M73-M88 including the eight 'Best 3rd place of XXXXX' pairings that match the frozen fixture slot tokens.

yellow_red_cards.summary: Card suspension carry-over rules. Single yellow cards in the final competition are cancelled after both the group stage and the quarter-finals.
yellow_red_cards.title: Yellow and red cards

document_title: Regulations for the FIFA World Cup 26
license: Copyright FIFA. Redistribution terms unclear, so the raw PDF is kept out of version control (data/raw/regulations/ is gitignored) per project constraint; this extracted manifest plus the reviewed fixtures are the committed evidence.
notes: Key finding: the official 2026 group-ranking fallback (Article 13 step 3) is the FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking editions, NOT drawing of lots. Drawing of lots appears in Article 11.2 only for group formation at the draw. The conduct-score table inside Article 13 (criterion f) is the ranking tie-break input; Annexe B's Fair Play Contest is a separate award. The PDF reports 98 physical pages; printed page numbers end at 84 while Annexe C continues to option 495.

reviewed_extraction.extracted_by: Claude (GSD executor, plan 03-01), reviewed against rendered page images of the same PDF
reviewed_extraction.method: Downloaded the official PDF over HTTPS from digitalhub.fifa.com with curl; computed SHA-256; read Articles 10-14 and Annexes A-C from rendered page images; parsed the 495 Annexe C option rows from physical pages 79-98 with pypdf using the strict row pattern '^\d{1,3}(3[A-L]){8}$'.
reviewed_extraction.verification: Parsed table passed: 495 contiguous options 1-495; 8 distinct groups per row; the 495 qualifying-group sets equal the complete C(12,8) enumeration with no duplicates; every assignment is contained in its fixture slot token; 14 rows (options 1, 2, 10, 18, 19, 46, 47, 48, 77, 105, 106, 127, 130, 131, 134, 495) cross-checked character-by-character against independently rendered page images with zero mismatches.

source: FIFA digital hub (official FIFA document repository)
